memberdef.name: ADDRESS_1_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* MSB byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: ADDRESS_2_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* Middle byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: ADDRESS_3_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 3 /* LSB byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: DUMMY_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 4 /* Dummy byte offset for fast, dual and quad reads */

memberdef.name: DUMMY_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* Number of dummy bytes for fast, dual and quad reads */

memberdef.name: RD_ID_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 4 /* Read ID command + 3 bytes ID response */

briefdescription.para: This function is the handler which performs processing for the QSPI driver.

detaileddescription.para: It is called from an interrupt context such that the amount of processing performed should be minimized. It is called when a transfer of QSPI data completes or an error occurs.
This handler provides an example of how to handle QSPI interrupts but is application specific.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference passed to the handler.
is the status of the QSPI .
is the number of bytes transferred.

briefdescription.para: This function erases the sectors in the serial FLASH connected to the QSPI interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
contains the address of the first sector which needs to be erased.
contains the total size to be erased.

memberdef.argsstring: (XQspiPs *QspiPtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 Command)
memberdef.name: FlashWrite

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the serial FLASH connected to the QSPI interface.

detaileddescription.para: All the data put into the buffer must be in the same page of the device with page boundaries being on 256 byte boundaries.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
contains the address to write data to in the FLASH.
contains the number of bytes to write.
is the command used to write data to the flash. QSPI device supports only Page Program command to write data to the flash.

memberdef.argsstring: (XQspiPs *QspiPtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 Command)
memberdef.name: FlashRead

briefdescription.para: This function reads from the serial FLASH connected to the QSPI interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.
contains the address to read data from in the FLASH.
contains the number of bytes to read.
is the command used to read data from the flash. QSPI device supports one of the Read, Fast Read, Dual Read and Fast Read commands to read data from the flash.

briefdescription.para: This function reads serial FLASH ID connected to the SPI interface.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if read id, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: This function enables quad mode in the serial flash connected to the SPI interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the QSPI driver component to use.

briefdescription.para: The purpose of this function is to illustrate how to use the XQspiPs device driver in interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes and reads data from a serial FLASH.

QspiInstancePtr
is a pointer to the QSPIPS driver to use. .
BaseAddress
contains the base address of the device
XST_SUCCESS if successful else XST_FAILURE.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration. This function calls other functions which contain loops that may be infinite if interrupts are not working such that it may not return. If the device slave select is not correct and the device is not responding on bus it will read a status of 0xFF for the status register as the bus is pulled up.

briefdescription.para: Main function to call the QSPI Flash example.
This file contains a design example using the QSPI driver (XQspiPs) in interrupt mode with a serial FLASH device.

detaileddescription.para: This examples performs some transfers in Manual Chip Select and Start mode. It is recommended to use Manual CS + Auto start for best performance. The hardware which this example runs on, must have a serial FLASH (Numonyx N25Q, Winbond W25Q, Spansion S25FL, ISSI IS25WP) for it to run. This example has been tested with the Numonyx Serial Flash (N25Q128) and IS25WP series flash parts.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sdm 11/25/10 First release 1.01 srt 06/12/12 Changed to meet frequency requirements of READ command for CR 663787 2.00a kka 22/08/12 Updated the example as XQspiPs_Transfer API has changed. Changed the prescalar to use divide by 8. The user can change the prescalar to a maximum of divide by 2 based on the reference clock in the system. Set the Holdb_dr bit in the configuration register using XQSPIPS_HOLD_B_DRIVE_OPTION. Setting this bit drives the HOLD bit of the QSPI controller. This is required for QSPI to be used in Non QSPI boot mode else there needs to be an external pullup on this line. See http://www.xilinx.com/support/answers/47596.htm 2.01a sg 02/03/13 Created a function FlashReadID. Removed multiple initialization using SetOptions. ms 04/05/17 Modified Comment lines in functions to recognize it as documentation block for doxygen generation. 3.5 tjs 07/16/18 Added support for low density ISSI flash parts. Added FlashQuadEnable API to enable quad mode in flash. 3.6 akm 04/15/19 Modified FlashQuadEnable, FlashWrie and FlashErase APIs, to wait for the on going operation to complete before performing the next operation. 3.10 akm 08/17/22 Fix logical error in NumSect calculation. 3.11 akm 07/10/23 Add support for system device-tree flow for example. 3.14 sb 03/25/25 Fixed gcc and g++ warnings. 3.15 vlt 12/16/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
